Conditions A to Z
Genital herpes 
Genital herpes is a sexually transmitted infection (STI) passed on through vaginal, anal and oral sex. Treatment from a sexual health clinic can help. Symptoms clear up on their own but can come back. 
Symptoms of genital herpes 
Symptoms of genital herpes include:
small blisters that burst to leave red, open sores around your genitals, anus, thighs or bottom – the sores may be less red on brown or black skin tingling, burning or itching around your genitals pain when you pee discharge that is not usual for you 
Where genital herpes symptoms appear and what they look like
Genital herpes can appear as a blister on the vagina.
Credit: DR P. MARAZZI/SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY https://www.sciencephoto.com/media/529674/view
Blisters can also appear on the penis in a group.
Credit: Clinical Photography/SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY https://www.sciencephoto.com/media/861692/view
You can also get blisters and sores on the buttocks.
Credit: DR P. MARAZZI/SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY https://www.sciencephoto.com/media/683453/view
Non-urgent advice: Go to a sexual health clinic as soon as possible if:
you have symptoms of genital herpes Go even if you have not
